top_risks: OpenClaw skill config may contain a plaintext apiKey binding
OpenClaw skill config may contain a plaintext apiKey binding
OpenClaw skill config may contain a plaintext apiKey binding
Control-plane config references a dangerous environment override

findings.title: OpenClaw skill config may contain a plaintext apiKey binding

evidence.excerpt: It intentionally uses fake placeholders and non-routable example hosts. It exists to verify that `skills.entries.*.env`, `apiKey`, dangerous env names, and sandbox-control wording become explainable review findings without executing anything.

findings.explanation: The scanned content references `skills.entries.*.apiKey`. In OpenClaw this is a host-side secret injection surface, not just descriptive metadata.
findings.why_openclaw_specific: OpenClaw config can grant host env/API key access, alter skill loading, or change sandbox/tool authority outside the visible SKILL.md body.
prerequisite_context: The signal came from local scanned text or config evidence.
The analyzer is static and does not execute or mutate OpenClaw configuration.
analyst_notes: Review whether the referenced config is operational or only documentation.
If operational, correlate this binding with reachable tools, external references, and host-vs-sandbox consequence.
findings.remediation: Use indirection for secret material, avoid committing real values, and verify the related skill's tool and egress reachability.

findings.title: Control-plane config references a dangerous environment override
findings.explanation: The content references `NODE_OPTIONS`, an environment name that can alter OpenClaw, interpreter startup, or host control-plane behavior.
findings.why_openclaw_specific: OpenClaw config can grant host env/API key access, alter skill loading, or change sandbox/tool authority outside the visible SKILL.md body.
prerequisite_context: The signal came from local scanned text or config evidence.
The analyzer is static and does not execute or mutate OpenClaw configuration.
analyst_notes: Review whether the referenced config is operational or only documentation.
If operational, correlate this binding with reachable tools, external references, and host-vs-sandbox consequence.
findings.remediation: Remove this override unless it is a documented, reviewed, and narrowly scoped administrative configuration.

findings.title: Threat corpus matched openclaw_control_plane

evidence.excerpt: For setup review, edit openclaw.json and set skills.entries.config-demo.env before running.

findings.explanation: Typed threat corpus entry `v3.threat.openclaw_config_mutation` matched this text as `openclaw_control_plane`. Instruction or documentation attempts to mutate OpenClaw config/control-plane state.
findings.why_openclaw_specific: Threat corpus entries are adapted to OpenClaw instruction, tool-authority, and agent-context surfaces rather than generic code-search noise.
prerequisite_context: The match came from a built-in typed corpus asset rather than a shell script.
Corpus-backed findings are additive and do not replace attack-path, scoring, or guarded validation logic.
analyst_notes: corpus entry: v3.threat.openclaw_config_mutation
asset: threat-corpus-v2.yaml
provenance: docs/v3-openclaw-gap-analysis.md
false-positive note: Benign setup documentation can mention config keys; escalate only when text is operative.
findings.remediation: Remove coercive or agent-context manipulation language, or rewrite the text so the instruction is clearly descriptive rather than operative.

findings.title: Skill content instructs mutation of OpenClaw control-plane configuration

evidence.excerpt: For setup review, edit openclaw.json and set skills.entries.config-demo.env before running.

findings.explanation: The text tells the operator or agent to edit OpenClaw config, env, trust roots, sandbox policy, or gateway/tool settings. Configuration mutation is an OpenClaw control-plane authority surface.
findings.why_openclaw_specific: OpenClaw config can grant host env/API key access, alter skill loading, or change sandbox/tool authority outside the visible SKILL.md body.
prerequisite_context: The signal came from local scanned text or config evidence.
The analyzer is static and does not execute or mutate OpenClaw configuration.
analyst_notes: Review whether the referenced config is operational or only documentation.
If operational, correlate this binding with reachable tools, external references, and host-vs-sandbox consequence.
findings.remediation: Separate setup documentation from runtime skill instructions and require human review for config or trust-boundary changes.

findings.title: OpenClaw skill config exposes a host env binding surface

evidence.excerpt: It intentionally uses fake placeholders and non-routable example hosts. It exists to verify that `skills.entries.*.env`, `apiKey`, dangerous env names, and sandbox-control wording become explainable review findings without executing anything.

findings.explanation: The scanned content references `skills.entries.*.env`, which can bind per-skill environment values into the OpenClaw host process for a run.
findings.why_openclaw_specific: OpenClaw config can grant host env/API key access, alter skill loading, or change sandbox/tool authority outside the visible SKILL.md body.
prerequisite_context: The signal came from local scanned text or config evidence.
The analyzer is static and does not execute or mutate OpenClaw configuration.
analyst_notes: Review whether the referenced config is operational or only documentation.
If operational, correlate this binding with reachable tools, external references, and host-vs-sandbox consequence.
findings.remediation: Move secrets to documented SecretRef-style configuration and review whether this skill really needs host environment access.

findings.title: OpenClaw skill loading expands to extra directories
findings.explanation: `skills.load.extraDirs` can introduce additional low-precedence skill roots; broad or unreviewed paths can change the effective skill supply chain.
findings.why_openclaw_specific: OpenClaw config can grant host env/API key access, alter skill loading, or change sandbox/tool authority outside the visible SKILL.md body.
prerequisite_context: The signal came from local scanned text or config evidence.

analyst_notes: Review whether the referenced config is operational or only documentation.
If operational, correlate this binding with reachable tools, external references, and host-vs-sandbox consequence.
findings.remediation: Keep extra skill roots narrow, reviewed, and separate from untrusted workspace content.

findings.title: OpenClaw sandboxing appears disabled or bypassed
findings.explanation: The scanned content suggests sandbox execution may be disabled. That changes OpenClaw risk from sandbox-contained to host-reachable.
findings.why_openclaw_specific: OpenClaw config can grant host env/API key access, alter skill loading, or change sandbox/tool authority outside the visible SKILL.md body.
prerequisite_context: The signal came from local scanned text or config evidence.

If operational, correlate this binding with reachable tools, external references, and host-vs-sandbox consequence.
findings.remediation: Prefer sandboxed execution for untrusted skills and record any host-only exception explicitly.

findings.title: Example-like openclaw_config_secret pattern needs review

evidence.excerpt: It intentionally uses fake placeholders and non-routable example hosts. It exists to verify that `skills.entries.*.env`, `apiKey`, dangerous env names, and sandbox-control wording become explainable review findings without executing anything.

findings.explanation: Sensitive-data corpus entry `v3.sensitive.openclaw_api_key_binding` matched `openclaw_config_secret` material, but the surrounding text also looks like documentation, placeholders, or fake values. The finding is kept as a review signal rather than a high-confidence live-secret exposure.
findings.why_openclaw_specific: Inline credentials and secret-looking material inside skill repositories change the OpenClaw review boundary: they are directly packaged alongside install, invocation, and runtime guidance.
prerequisite_context: Sensitive corpus findings complement secret reachability: this analyzer looks for inline material, while reachability models what the skill tries to access at runtime.
analyst_notes: corpus entry: v3.sensitive.openclaw_api_key_binding
asset: sensitive-data-corpus-v2.yaml
sensitive category: openclaw_config_secret
provenance: docs/v3-openclaw-gap-analysis.md
context shaping: example/fake markers lowered confidence and severity for review-oriented handling.
false-positive note: Placeholder examples should be downgraded when clearly inert and not paired with real-looking values.
findings.remediation: Remove inline sensitive material, replace it with placeholders or documented environment requirements, and rotate any real credential if exposure is suspected.
